name: Current Affairs in History & Heritage (1)

sourceRefs.label: vajiramandravi.com/pwonlyias.com - India's UNESCO World Heritage Sites count and recent additions (2025-26)

researchNotes: Third topic in the Part A Current-Affairs rotation (Unit III's turn). All counts/figures are explicitly tied to 2025-2026 reporting and will be superseded as new sites get inscribed — flagged accordingly rather than presented as fixed facts. Expansion pass (Batch 12): added Gingee Fort's TN connection to the Maratha Military Landscapes site, 2025 ASI discoveries (Kerala megaliths, TN iron objects, Rajasthan Harappan find), the Underwater Archaeology Wing, the Sarnath nomination, and ASI's founding — broadening beyond the original UNESCO-count-only content.

reading.intro: Covers India's UNESCO World Heritage Site count and recent additions/recognitions (2025-2026), directly relevant to the syllabus's history/heritage and 'Current affairs' clauses.

qa.question: How many UNESCO World Heritage Sites does India have, as of 2026, and how does India rank globally?
qa.answer: 44 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, as of 2026 — ranking India 6th globally in total UNESCO sites.

qa.question: What was the most recent addition to India's UNESCO World Heritage Sites, and when/where was it inscribed?
qa.answer: The Maratha Military Landscapes — officially inscribed in July 2025, at the 47th session of the World Heritage Committee in Paris.

qa.question: How are India's 44 UNESCO World Heritage Sites categorised?
qa.answer: 36 cultural sites, 7 natural sites, and 1 MIXED site (Khangchendzonga National Park) — mixed meaning it qualifies under both cultural and natural criteria.

qa.question: What recent addition was made to UNESCO's Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity from India, and what is India's total count on that list?
qa.answer: Deepavali (Diwali) was added in 2025. This brings India's total to 16 elements on UNESCO's Representative List of Intangible Cultural Heritage.

qa.question: What happened to India's UNESCO Tentative List in 2025?
qa.answer: Six new sites were added (including Kanger Valley National Park in Chhattisgarh and the Mudumal Megalithic Menhirs in Telangana), raising India's total Tentative List count to 62 sites.

qa.question: How many monuments does the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) currently conserve and maintain, including how many World Heritage properties?
qa.answer: 3,686 monuments in total, including 27 World Heritage properties.

qa.question: Which Tamil Nadu fort is included within the 'Maratha Military Landscapes' World Heritage Site, and why is this significant?
qa.answer: Gingee Fort — it is the only one of the 12 forts in the site located OUTSIDE Maharashtra (the other 11 are in Maharashtra), making it Tamil Nadu's connection to India's newest UNESCO site.

qa.question: What major megalithic discovery did the ASI make in Kerala in March 2025?
qa.answer: Over 110 megaliths spread across 45 hectares in Palakkad, mostly built using granite slabs and boulders — expected to shed light on early Iron Age society in Kerala.

qa.question: What did archaeologists discover at six sites in Tamil Nadu in February 2025, and how old were the finds?
qa.answer: Iron objects, believed to be between 5,000 and 5,400 years old — findings that push back the antiquity of ironworking in the Tamil region and feed directly into the ongoing Sangam-era/Keeladi antiquity debate.

qa.question: What Harappan-related discovery was made in Rajasthan in early 2025, and why is it significant?
qa.answer: Harappan remains were discovered in Jaisalmer, near the Pakistan border — the FIRST time artefacts from the 4,500-year-old Indus Valley Civilization have been found in the Thar desert, proving the civilization's geographical footprint extended further than previously known.

qa.question: What has the ASI expanded in 2025 to explore submerged historical sites, and along which coasts?
qa.answer: Its Underwater Archaeology Wing — focusing on coastal waters in Maharashtra, Gujarat, and Tamil Nadu to find submerged historical sites.

qa.question: Which Indian site is slated for UNESCO World Heritage inscription in the 2025-26 cycle, per current nominations?
qa.answer: The Ancient Buddhist Site, Sarnath (Uttar Pradesh) — with the JingkiengJri/Lyu Charai Cultural Landscape (Meghalaya) slated for the following 2026-27 cycle.

qa.question: Who founded the Archaeological Survey of India, and when?
qa.answer: Alexander Cunningham founded the ASI in 1861 and served as its first Director-General — he is known as the 'Father of Indian Archaeology'.
